assessor_1_raw: Card fraud detection experiment: well-designed with appropriate temporal split, chargeback-confirmed labels (90-day resolution), and precision at fixed recall as primary metric aligning with business objective. AUPRC as secondary metric addresses single operating point limitation. Ablations for merchant embeddings and augmented baseline are excellent confound controls. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: Strong fraud detection experiment. Temporal split is mandatory for fraud (causal ordering). Chargeback-confirmed labels eliminate label noise. Both models receive equivalent tuning. Attention weights and SHAP values for interpretability are appropriate for fraud systems. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: The design correctly uses precision at fixed recall as the primary metric — this directly maps to the investigator capacity constraint. AUPRC, stratified performance by fraud amount, and temporal stratification are comprehensive. The ablation suite (embedding ablation, augmented baseline) is thorough. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: All three assessors unanimous: defense_wins. Consistent evaluation across all runs.
